Interval-censored PAMMs: Monte Carlo benchmark

Published

June 10, 2026

All prose numbers in this report are computed from results/summary.rds (2246967 raw result rows, up to 500 replications per cell). Monte Carlo SEs for grid-averaged coverage are clustered at the replication level. Cells where a method is misspecified by design are flagged with open symbols.

Failure rates

2 (cell, method) combinations had failures; max failure rate 0.4% (cr-cr-cr-random-r0.3-n450-m10, mi).

cell_id method n_rep n_fail fail_rate first_error
cr-cr-cr-random-r0.3-n450-m10 mi 500 2 0.004 Failed to draw an interval-censored event time for known cause 2 after 1000 rejection-sampling proposals. The fitted cause-specific hazard is too small relative to the all-cause hazard in the censoring interval.
cr-cr-cr-random-r0.6-n450-m10 mi 500 1 0.002 Failed to draw an interval-censored event time for known cause 2 after 1000 rejection-sampling proposals. The fitted cause-specific hazard is too small relative to the all-cause hazard in the censoring interval.

Fig 1 (headline): coverage of S(t|x) and interval-average hazard

MI survival coverage across core cells: 0.540-0.950 (median 0.918). Midpoint: 0.376-0.937 (median 0.766).

Fig 2: beta / logHR(t) coverage + paired MI−midpoint differences

Fig 3: point accuracy (RMSE, incl. Turnbull)

Median RRMSE vs oracle: MI 1.23, midpoint 1.55, Turnbull 1.71; Turnbull beats MI in 4/24 core cells (it is a step-function NPMLE without smoothing). Turnbull mean equivalence-class half-gap (scoring ambiguity) ranges 0.000-0.231 across core cells.

Fig 4: CI width and avSE/empSE (the mechanism)

Fig 5: time-varying effect recovery

  • const baseline: least-false constant logHR (exact-time convention): Cox 0.687, Weibull 0.695
  • peaked baseline: least-false constant logHR (exact-time convention): Cox 0.539, Weibull 0.550

Fig 6: competing risks, smooth f(x), m-sensitivity
